LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO CHRONICLE, A DIVISION OF HEARST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT (1) AN ESSENTIAL FUNCTION OF PLAINTIFF'S JOB WAS TO LIFT THE BUNDLES; (2) PLAINTIFF COULD NOT PERFORM HIS JOB, INCLUDING THE LIFTING, THROUGH MARCH 2004; (3) DEFENDANT ATTEMPTED TO CONTACT PLAINTIFF IN MARCH 2004 REGARDING A REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION; (4) PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROPERLY RESPOND TO DEFENDANT'S INQUIRIES; (5) PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ASK FOR OR SHOW HOW DEFENDANT COULD HAVE ACCOMMODATED PLAINTIFF; AND (6) DEFENDANT PROPERLY TERMINATED PLAINTIFF BECAUSE HE COULD NOT PERFORM THE ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF HIS JOB. ALSO, THE COURT ADDED TO THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: REGARDING THE BUYOUT ISSUE, PLAINTIFF DID NOT APPLY FOR A BUYOUT PACKAGE (NUMBER #34 DEFENDANT'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS) (BOTH SIDES GIVEN ENDORSED FILED COPIES OF ORDER REGARDING EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS BEFORE HEARING). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER RE: DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2009 Set For Case Management Conference On Apr-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO CHRONICLE, A DIVISION OF HEARST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Jimmy Domino, (707) 297-1170, Plaintiff, in Pro Per, and opposing party; Dennis C. Huie, Esq., and Aaron M. Scolari, Esq., of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP, (415) 402-0059, appearing for Defendant, San Francisco Chronicle, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion Granted. The undisputed evidence shows that (1) an essential function of plaintiff's job was to lift the bundles; (2) plaintiff could not perform his job, including the lifting, through March 2004; (3) defendant attempted to contact plaintiff in March 2004 regarding a reasonable accommodation; (4) plaintiff did not properly respond to defendant's inquiries; (5) plaintiff failed to ask for or show how defendant could have accommodated plaintiff; and (6) defendant properly terminated plaintiff because he could not perform the essential functions of his job. Also, the Court added to the tentative ruling as follows: Regarding the buyout issue, Plaintiff did not apply for a buyout package (Number #34 Defendant's Statement of undisputed facts) (Both sides given endorsed filed copies of Order regarding evidentiary objections before hearing). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

Ntc And Mtn To Quash Defts Subpoena Duces Tecum
Off Calendar Sep-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to quash the subpoena the duces tecum is ordered off calendar. Code of Civil Procedure Section 1005 requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed and served by mail on August 22, 2008. In addition, the proof of service is defective. Code of Civil Procedure Section 1013a requires that service shall be made by a person who is not a party to the action. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition on 9/24/08, 10:00AM and further ordered to comply with 8/27/08 order. Monetary sanctions of $700 payable within 30 days. Supplemental briefing as to the motion for terminating sanctions shall be submitted by 10 am, 9/26/08. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
